headline: CrowdStrike ARR Tops US$5b As Identity And AI Security Expand
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings (NasdaqGS:CRWD) reported a record fiscal year, reaching more than $5b in annual recurring revenue. The company launched FalconID, a next generation, passwordless authentication product with AI resistant multifactor authentication. CrowdStrike announced a partnership with VAST Data and NVIDIA focused on securing data across the full AI workflow. CrowdStrike enters this phase with its shares at $391.42 and a long run track record that includes a 3 year return of 213.3%...

headline: UK self-driving startup Oxa raises $103mln to scale up at ports, airports
summary: British self-driving startup Oxa has raised $103 million from investors to scale up autonomous ‌vehicle operations focused on ports, airports, warehouses and ‌other industrial applications, it said on Wednesday.  Oxa, based in Oxford, central England, ​said that $50 million of its Series D funding round came from the UK's National Wealth Fund and included investments from chipmaker Nvidia's venture capital arm NVentures and BP's bp Ventures.  Unlike ‌robotaxi developers intent on ⁠passenger cars, Oxa is focused on what founder Paul Newman calls "industrial mobile autonomy" where there ⁠is less complexity because there is less traffic and fewer encounters with pedestrians.

content.title: UK self-driving startup Oxa raises $103mln to scale up at ports, airports
content.summary: British self-driving startup Oxa has raised $103 million from investors to scale up autonomous ‌vehicle operations focused on ports, airports, warehouses and ‌other industrial applications, it said on Wednesday.  Oxa, based in Oxford, central England, ​said that $50 million of its Series D funding round came from the UK's National Wealth Fund and included investments from chipmaker Nvidia's venture capital arm NVentures and BP's bp Ventures.  Unlike ‌robotaxi developers intent on ⁠passenger cars, Oxa is focused on what founder Paul Newman calls "industrial mobile autonomy" where there ⁠is less complexity because there is less traffic and fewer encounters with pedestrians.

headline: Nvidia Backs Another UK Driverless Startup in $103 Million Round
summary: The US chipmaker’s venture capital arm NVentures joined a funding round for Oxa that raised $103 million, the British company said Wednesday.  Oxa declined to comment on its valuation in the round or how much Nvidia invested.

content.title: Nvidia Backs Another UK Driverless Startup in $103 Million Round
content.summary: The US chipmaker’s venture capital arm NVentures joined a funding round for Oxa that raised $103 million, the British company said Wednesday.  Oxa declined to comment on its valuation in the round or how much Nvidia invested.
